documentation.readme: Empire Empire is a post-exploitation and adversary emulation framework that is used to aid Red Teams and Penetration Testers. The Empire server is written in Python 3 and is modular to allow operator flexibility. Empire comes built-in with a client that can be used remotely to access the server. There is also a GUI available for remotely accessing the Empire server, Starkiller.
usage_sections: Features: - Server/Client Architecture for Multiplayer Support - Fully encrypted communications - HTTP/S, Malleable HTTP, OneDrive, Dropbox, and PHP Listeners - Massive library (400+) of supported tools in PowerShell, C#, & Python - Donut Integration for shellcode generation - Modular plug

Quickstart: When cloning this repository, you will need to recurse submodules. Check out the Installation Page for install instructions. Note: The main branch is a reflection of the latest changes and may not always be stable. After cloning the repo, you can checkout the latest stable releas

arguments.name: --clean
arguments.description: Drop and reinitialize the database. Removes Starkiller/Empire-Compiler files.

arguments.name: --config
arguments.description: Specify a config.yaml different from the config.yaml in the empire/server directory.

arguments.name: -d
arguments.description: Set the logging level to DEBUG

arguments.name: --debug
arguments.description: Set the logging level to DEBUG

arguments.name: -install
arguments.description: plugin '{entry.name}' v{entry.version} installed"

arguments.name: -l
arguments.description: Set the logging level

arguments.name: --log-level
arguments.description: Set the logging level

arguments.name: --reset
arguments.description: Drop and reinitialize the database. Keep config and Starkiller/Empire-Compiler files intact.

arguments.name: -v
arguments.description: Display current Empire version.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Display current Empire version.

arguments.name: -y
arguments.description: Automatically say yes to all prompts during installation

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

name: Empire
summary: Empire Empire is a post-exploitation and adversary emulation framework that is used to aid Red Teams and Penetration Testers. The Empire server is written in Python 3 and is modular to allow operator flexibility. Empire comes built-in with a client that can be used remotely to access the server. trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need frameworks, use Empire because Empire Empire is a post-exploitation and adversary emulation framework that is used to aid Red Teams and Penetration Testers. The Empire server is written in Py.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./setup/checkout-latest-tag.sh` as documented upstream for Empire.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./ps-empire install -y` as documented upstream for Empire.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./ps-empire server` as documented upstream for Empire.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./ps-empire server -h` as documented upstream for Empire.
When chaining frameworks tools, pair Empire with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `Empire --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
